title: Save $575 on this OLED Asus gaming laptop with RTX 5070, now just $1,575 ROG Zephyrus G16 comes with 240 Hz refresh rate, Intel Core Ultra 9, 16GB of RAM, and a 1TB SSD
summary: Save $575 on the ROG Zephyrus G16 gaming laptop, now priced at $1,575. This Asus laptop features a 240 Hz refresh rate, Intel Core Ultra 9 processor, 16GB of RAM, and a 1TB SSD.
insight: High-performance gaming laptop now more affordable with significant price reduction

title: Linux prepares to axe legacy x32 hybrid mode hybrid 32-bit/64-bit mode faces complete removal by 2027 due to low adoption
summary: Linux developers plan to remove the x32 ABI due to low adoption, a hybrid mode that combines 32-bit and 64-bit capabilities. The x32 ABI was introduced as a potential solution for 32-bit binary compatibility on 64-bit systems. Its removal is expected by 2027.
insight: Legacy x32 ABI faces removal due to lack of developer adoption

title: New Silicon Motion SM2524XT chip brings 14 GB/s to mainstream SSDs 6nm DRAMless controller boasts heavy AI PC optimization and slashes KV cache latency
summary: Silicon Motion's SM2524XT mainstream SSD controller achieves 14 GB/s read speed and 2.5 million random IOPS, making it suitable for AI-optimized PCs and heavy workloads.
insight: Boosts AI PC performance with high-speed storage solutions

title: Independent cyber audit finds zero malware or backdoors in DJI drones U.S. firm's hardware analysis challenges FCC ban amid ongoing $1.56 billion legal battle
summary: DJI publishes results of independent security assessment by OnDefend, finding no malware or backdoors in their drones.
insight: Independent audit challenges FCC ban on DJI drones over national security concerns.

title: Ambernic's retro gaming handhelds are quietly losing RAM capacity and being downgraded to older LPDDR3 memory company says 1GB capacity is still the standard, 512MB models 'an unexpected error'
summary: Anbernic retro gaming handhelds are being shipped with reduced RAM capacity without customer notification, downgrading from LPDDR4 to LPDDR3 memory in some units.
insight: Reduced RAM capacity affects gaming performance and device functionality.

title: IBM ThinkPad T43 enthusiast installs 'almost' every version of Windows on the single-core laptop without using virtual machine 26 years of Windows running bare metal, from 1996 Windows NT 4.0 to Windows 10 22H2 working on legendary hardware
summary: An IBM ThinkPad user has successfully installed various Windows versions bare-metal on their single-core laptop from 1996 to 2023, demonstrating exceptional technical skills and dedication.
insight: Legacy hardware can still run modern operating systems with proper configuration and effort.

title: Startup unveils underwater drone designed for protecting undersea cables small drone dives to 1,640 feet, comes with four-hour battery endurance or unlimited range when tethered to surface power source
summary: A British-New Zealand startup has unveiled an AI-powered undersea drone for patrolling undersea infrastructure, powered by an autonomous software stack. The drone can dive to 1,640 feet and has four-hour battery endurance or unlimited range when tethered. It allows a single operator to coordinate multiple drones.
insight: Enhanced underwater security with AI-powered drone surveillance capabilities.

title: Mystery company accidentally blew $500 million on Claude AI in a single month failed to put usage limit on licenses for employees
summary: A company accidentally spent $500 million on Claude AI in a single month due to a lack of usage limits on employee licenses.
insight: Accidental AI overspending can have catastrophic financial implications for businesses.

title: Microsoft warns GPU mining malware is being spread to users through SEO poisoning and AI chatbots cryptojacking campaign targets gamers and high-end PC users with downloads disguised as popular PC utilities
summary: Microsoft uncovered a GPU-focused cryptojacking campaign using SEO poisoning and chatbots to spread malware disguised as popular PC utilities.
insight: Users are vulnerable to malware through trusted software recommendations and search results.

title: Supermicro says it assisted Taiwanese authorities in server smuggling bust that led to three arrests company issues statement on working with US, Taiwan to block illicit diversion of servers to China
summary: Supermicro assisted Taiwanese authorities in a server smuggling bust, leading to 3 arrests and 50 servers seized.
insight: Counter-smuggling efforts thwart Chinese access to sensitive US tech

title: 'Call of Duty Modern Warfare 4' developer promises PC focus Infinity Ward promises extensive optimization and is dropping older consoles
summary: Infinity Ward is dropping older consoles and focusing on PC optimization for Call of Duty Modern Warfare 4. The developer promises extensive optimization and PC-specific options for a smoother gaming experience. This shift prioritizes performance and features for PC gamers.
insight: Better console drop means more resources for PC optimization and features

title: Trailing-edge foundry roadmaps for GlobalFoundries, UMC, and SMIC mature node chipmakers each pursue differing strategies and IP
summary: GlobalFoundries, UMC, and SMIC pursue distinct trailing-edge roadmaps shaped by geography, regulation, and technology choices, with varying strategies in IP and manufacturing processes.
insight: Differing strategies impact the future of global semiconductor manufacturing ecosystems.

title: TP-Link announces its first consumer Wi-Fi 8 roadmap Archer 8 routers scheduled to arrive in October 2026, pending FCC approval
summary: TP-Link announced its Wi-Fi 8 roadmap with Archer 8 router launch in October, pending FCC approval.
insight: Boosts wireless speeds and efficiency for modern smart homes and devices.

title: Leaks reveal US authorities concerned about the rise of anti-tech extremists as AI data center issues become increasingly contentious critics say this could lead to surveillance, criminalization of peaceful opposition
summary: A leaked report reveals law enforcement concerns about anti-tech extremist protests, warning of widespread violence and unrest. The protests are against data centers and AI, with critics arguing they could lead to surveillance and criminalization of peaceful opposition. The situation has become increasingly contentious, with authorities taking a stern stance.
insight: Government surveillance and censorship may silence AI critics and protesters.

title: Researchers say they can spy on your browsing by measuring SSD activity through a browser API claim FROST attack requires no permissions or user interaction to identify which apps and websites you're using
summary: Researchers claim to have developed FROST, an attack that can spy on users' browsing activity through a browser API without permission or user interaction. FROST exploits the Origin Private File System (OPFS), allowing websites to create files on the local disk. This allows for indirect tracking of user behavior.
insight: Browser API can bypass permission requirements for data snooping attacks

title: Intel challenges AMD s handheld dominance with new Arc G3 chips Panther Lake silicon brings up to 14 cores, Arc B390 graphics to handhelds
summary: Intel has unveiled the Arc G3 and G3 Extreme chips, built on Panther Lake dies, featuring up to 14 cores and an Arc B390 iGPU, set to power devices from Acer and OneXPlayer.
insight: Intel challenges AMD's handheld graphics dominance with new Arc G3 chips

title: Qualcomm announces Snapdragon C Platform for $300 and up laptops Windows on Arm and NPUs for the budget tier
summary: Qualcomm announced the Snapdragon C Platform for budget laptops priced at $300 and up, featuring a new Kryo chip variant. This platform aims to power laptops with Windows on Arm and Neural Processing Units (NPUs). It targets the budget tier, competing with existing affordable laptop options.
insight: Qualcomm enters budget laptop market with competitive Kryo chip variant

title: Asus ROG Crosshair 2006 motherboard review: 20 years of ROG
summary: The Asus ROG Crosshair 2006 motherboard offers a premium mid-range AM5 experience with unique retro flair, robust power delivery, and high-speed networking options.
insight: Limited production run and nostalgia factor justify premium price increase.

title: Acer brings Intel Arc B390 graphics to Predator Atlas 8 gaming handheld G3 Extreme CPU paired with segment-first metal fan for increased airflow
summary: Acer's Predator Atlas 8 gaming handheld now features Intel Arc processors, including the G3 Extreme CPU paired with a segment-first metal fan for increased airflow, and options with Arc B370 or B390 iGPUs.
insight: Acer challenges AMD dominance in handheld gaming with Intel Arc integration.

title: Acer and Qualcomm take on MacBook Neo with first Snapdragon C laptop Aspire Go 15 delivers 512GB SSD and 8GB of RAM at entry-tier price
summary: Acer's Aspire Go 15 is the first Snapdragon C-powered laptop, offering 512GB storage and 8GB RAM at a competitive entry-tier price.
insight: Budget-friendly laptops with long-lasting batteries are gaining mainstream traction slowly.

title: Petros CH32H417M Alef A Raspberry Pi Pico-sized RISC-V USB 3.0 camera board
summary: The Petros CH32H417M Alef is a compact, Raspberry Pi Pico-sized board using WCH's CH32H417M RISC-V MCU, featuring a 2MP camera and USB 3.0 connectivity.
insight: Low-power, high-performance RISC-V board for AI and IoT applications.

title: u-blox ALMA-B2 Bluetooth 6.0 and 802.15.4 module features Nordic nRF54LM20 Edge AI wireless MCU
summary: u-blox announced the ALMA-B2 module series with Nordic nRF54LM20 wireless MCU, supporting BLE 6.0, 802.15.4, and Edge AI capabilities.
insight: Enables fast, energy-efficient Edge AI applications in wireless devices.

title: Qualcomm Snapdragon C to power cheaper, entry-level Arm Windows laptops selling for $300 and up
summary: Qualcomm Snapdragon C SoC powers entry-level Arm Windows laptops at $300+, targeting Intel competitors.
insight: Qualcomm enters budget laptop market with energy-efficient, AI-accelerated Arm Windows offerings.

title: AMD launches Versal Prime Gen 2 2VM3454, 2VM3254, and 2VM3104 adaptive SoCs in compact 23x23mm packages
summary: AMD has launched three new Versal Prime Gen 2 adaptive SoCs, the 2VM3454, 2VM3254, and 2VM3104, designed for space-constrained applications. These devices offer up to 100K DMIPS of scalar compute in compact 23x23mm packages. They deliver an optimized footprint and processing subsystem compared to earlier models.
insight: Significant performance boost in compact package for space-constrained applications.

title: AAEON MIX-PTLWV1 Panther Lake industrial mini-ITX motherboard offers quad DisplayPort, dual 10GbE, and more
summary: The AAEON MIX-PTLWV1 is an industrial mini-ITX motherboard featuring Intel Panther Lake processors with quad DisplayPort and dual 10GbE connectivity. It supports up to 128GB DDR5 memory, M.2 NVMe SSD storage, and more. The motherboard is designed for industrial applications requiring high-performance and expandability.
insight: High-performance industrial computing for demanding applications and environments

title: Apple may need Google's Gemini to make Siri smart enough to compete
summary: Apple's on-device AI capabilities are limited by memory and processing ceilings, hindering Siri's development. Large AI models now operate at a scale that Apple's custom silicon struggles to match. This gap may require Apple to collaborate with Google.
insight: On-device AI limitations threaten Apple's Siri competitive edge.

title: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket explodes in massive fireball during testing in Florida
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket exploded during a static fire test in Florida, marking a setback for the company's launch ambitions. The test was one of the final checks before launch and was meant to verify the rocket's engines and ground systems. The incident was captured on livestream footage.
insight: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket development faces significant technical hurdles.

title: YouTube is cracking down on undisclosed AI videos, but won't penalize creators for making them
summary: YouTube introduces a simplified labeling system for AI-generated content, emphasizing transparency.
insight: Creators won't face penalties for using AI, just disclosure.

title: Researchers found a way to spy on your browsing by watching your SSD's activity
summary: Researchers discovered a method to spy on browsing habits by monitoring SSD timing differences.
insight: SSD timing can reveal sensitive browsing information without direct access.

title: Uber burned through its entire 2026 AI budget in four months and has little to show for it
summary: Uber has burned through its 2026 AI budget in four months with little return. The company's AI spending is rising but hasn't seen meaningful ROI. President and Chief Operating Officer Andrew Macdonald notes this concern.
insight: Uber's AI investment is struggling to yield meaningful financial returns.

title: Redmagic 11S Pro gaming phone gets cooling and CPU upgrades, but drops to 16GB RAM as price climbs to $849
summary: The Redmagic 11S Pro gaming phone gets cooling and CPU upgrades, but drops to 16GB RAM, with a price increase to $849. The Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 Leading Version powers the device. Nubia focuses on squeezing more performance from familiar hardware.
insight: Performance upgrade prioritized over RAM capacity in new gaming phone release

title: Windows can now share audio to two Bluetooth headphones at once, sort of
summary: Windows now allows simultaneous audio transmission to two Bluetooth headphones using shared audio.
insight: Enhanced productivity and gaming experiences through multi-device audio connectivity.

title: Ultima Underworld returns as a fan-made Unity remake after 10 years of work
summary: A fan-made Unity remake of Ultima Underworld is finally released after 10 years of development. This project aims to recreate the classic gaming experience using the Unity engine. The remake is a labor of love from the gaming community.
insight: Fans bring classic gaming experience back to life with modern tech.

title: This startup has raised $12 million to make liquid-metal circuit boards rewritable
summary: Itera has raised $12 million to develop rewritable liquid-metal circuit boards using electrowetting technology. This approach dynamically forms connections by shifting conductive liquid across a glass substrate, effectively redrawing circuit paths. The technology has potential applications in fields such as electronics and medical devices.
insight: Rewritable circuit boards revolutionize electronics manufacturing and repair.

title: Valve's Steam Machine may cost more than today's Steam Deck OLED
summary: Brad Lynch warned that Valve's Steam Machine may cost more than the Steam Deck OLED. The Steam Deck OLED's price is around $399, making the potential increase concerning. This news may impact gamers' purchasing decisions.
insight: Potential price hike may deter gamers from buying Steam Machine.

title: Philips and AOC join the 1,000 Hz monitor race with new 1080p models
summary: AOC and Philips unveiled 1,000Hz 1080p monitors in collaboration with BOE, targeting esports and motion clarity.
insight: High refresh rate enhances competitive gaming and motion smoothness significantly.

title: MSI celebrates 40th anniversary with dragon-themed Titan laptop and MEG Ace motherboard
summary: MSI celebrates 40th anniversary with a special edition Titan laptop and MEG Ace motherboard. The Titan 18 HX Dragon Edition Draco Epic features a unique dragon design on its lid. This is a nod to the company's logo and anniversary.
insight: MSI's 40th anniversary edition showcases brand heritage and innovation.

title: Elizabeth Warren wants to tax AI data centers as power bills climb
summary: Elizabeth Warren proposes taxing AI data centers due to rising power costs affecting consumers.
insight: AI economic gains are concentrated among a few firms and executives.

title: Tech executives are suffering from "AI psychosis," says Box CEO
summary: Box CEO Aaron Levie labels the C-suite affliction as 'AI psychosis.' This condition refers to the anxiety or confusion caused by the rapid integration of AI technology into business operations. Levie believes it affects top executives.
insight: Over-reliance on AI can lead to decision-making uncertainty in executives.
